issues: outline_absent: peers have defined 1px dark outlines; candidate edges are organic fire pixels with no dark border
outline_color_mismatch: peers use black/near-black outline; candidate has no outline color at all — fire edge only
shadow_treatment_diverges: peers use directional drop-shadow; candidate has glow/bloom self-illumination with no cast shadow
pixel_scale_mismatch: candidate individual pixels appear 2–3x larger than peer sprite pixels at matched canvas scale — density inconsistent
color_temperature_extreme: candidate is all-warm orange/yellow/white fire palette; peer set is mixed-cool (greens, blues, purples dominate) — near-zero palette overlap
highlight_intensity_too_high: central sphere has very bright white specular; peers have subdued, low-contrast highlights
rendering_style_diverges: candidate has a soft gradient-within-pixels painted feel on the sphere; peers are flat classic Denzi pixel art
reasoning: The peer reference sheet is a classic Denzi-style sprite set: small, tight pixel art characters and creatures with consistent dark outlines, directional drop shadows, subdued highlights, and a mixed-cool color palette. The candidate vfx_explosion_3_v01 fails on multiple structural dimensions: (1) no dark outline anywhere — the fire edge is organic and outline-free; (2) no directional shadow — replaced by warm glow that is stylistically alien to the peer set; (3) highlight intensity on the central sphere is dramatically higher than any peer sprite; (4) the pixel grid scale appears coarser than peers at matched display size; (5) the warm orange/yellow/white fire palette shares almost no hue overlap with the predominantly green/purple/blue/neutral peer palette. On the positive side, the candidate is drawn in pixel art, has a charming face motif embedded in the explosion center (charm level +), and the overall silhouette is readable. However, the rendering-style and structural-style gaps are wide enough that it reads as a different artistic lineage placed next to Denzi sprites. Confidence reflects clear visibility of the comparison image.
